{{Short description|none}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Use mdy dates|date=November 2025}} {{Use American English|date=November 2025}} The knockout stage of the [[2026 FIFA World Cup]] is the second and final stage of the competition, following the [[2026 FIFA World Cup#Group stage|group stage]]. Played from June 28 to July 19, 2026, the knockout stage will end with the [[2026 FIFA World Cup final|final]], to be held at [[MetLife Stadium]] in [[East Rutherford, New Jersey|East Rutherford]], [[New Jersey]].{{cite web |url=https://digitalhub.fifa.com/m/1be9ce37eb98fcc5/original/FWC26-Match-Schedule_English.pdf |title=FIFA World Cup 26 – Match Schedule |publisher=[[FIFA]] |date=February 4, 2024 |access-date=February 4, 2024}} The top two teams from each of the twelve groups, along with the [[2026 FIFA World Cup#Ranking of third-place teams|best eight third-place teams]], have advanced to the knockout stage to compete in a [[single-elimination tournament]]. There are 32 matches in the knockout stage, including a match for third place.{{cite web |url=https://digitalhub.fifa.com/m/636f5c9c6f29771f/original/FWC2026_regulations_EN.pdf |title=FIFA World Cup 2026 Regulations |publisher=FIFA |date=May 2025 |access-date=June 7, 2025 |archive-date=June 23, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250623212158/https://digitalhub.fifa.com/m/636f5c9c6f29771f/original/FWC2026_regulations_EN.pdf |url-status=live}} ==Format== The knockout stage of the [[2026 FIFA World Cup]] will be contested between 32 teams that qualify from the group stage. Matches in the knockout stage will be played to a finish. If the score of a match is level at the end of 90 minutes of playing time, 30 minutes of [[Overtime (sports)#Association football|extra time]] will be played. If the scores are still tied, a [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalty shootout]] will determine the team that prevails. All times listed are local. In the round of 32, four of the group winners played group runners-up. The other eight group winners played advancing third-place teams. The eight remaining group runners-up played against each other. FIFA set out the following schedule for the round of 32: *'''Match 73''': Runner-up Group A vs Runner-up Group B *'''Match 74''': Winner Group E vs Third-place Group A/B/C/D/F *'''Match 75''': Winner Group F vs Runner-up Group C *'''Match 76''': Winner Group C vs Runner-up Group F *'''Match 77''': Winner Group I vs Third-place Group C/D/F/G/H *'''Match 78''': Runner-up Group E vs Runner-up Group I *'''Match 79''': Winner Group A vs Third-place Group C/E/F/H/I *'''Match 80''': Winner Group L vs Third-place Group E/H/I/J/K *'''Match 81''': Winner Group D vs Third-place Group B/E/F/I/J *'''Match 82''': Winner Group G vs Third-place Group A/E/H/I/J *'''Match 83''': Runner-up Group K vs Runner-up Group L *'''Match 84''': Winner Group H vs Runner-up Group J *'''Match 85''': Winner Group B vs Third-place Group E/F/G/I/J *'''Match 86''': Winner Group J vs Runner-up Group H *'''Match 87''': Winner Group K vs Third-place Group D/E/I/J/L *'''Match 88''': Runner-up Group D vs Runner-up Group G ===Combinations of matches in the round of 32=== The specific matchups involving the third-place teams depended on which eight third-place teams qualified for the round of 32. The 495 possible combinations were published in Annex C of the tournament regulations. The following eight groups had their third-place teams finish in the [[2026 FIFA World Cup#Ranking of third-place teams|top eight of the ranking]]: B, D, E, F, I, J, K, and L. According to the combinations table (option #67), the matchups involving third-place teams were therefore as follows: *1A (Mexico) vs 3E (Ecuador) *1B (Switzerland) vs 3J (Algeria) *1D (United States) vs 3B (Bosnia and Herzegovina) *1E (Germany) vs 3D (Paraguay) *1G (Belgium) vs 3I (Senegal) *1I (France) vs 3F (Sweden) *1K (Colombia) vs 3L (Ghana) *1L (England) vs 3K (DR Congo) The full combination table was as follows: {{2026 FIFA World Cup third-place table}} ==Qualified teams== The top two teams from each of the twelve groups, along with the eight best-ranked third-placed teams, qualified for the knockout stage. {| class="wikitable" style="white-space:nowrap;" |- ! ==Round of 16== ===Canada vs Morocco=== The teams had faced each other four times previously, with Morocco being unbeaten in any of these matches. Their most recent match was in the [[2022 FIFA World Cup Group F#Canada vs Morocco|group stage of the previous World Cup]], which Morocco won 2–1.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/canada/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Morocco/|title=Canada national football team: record v Morocco|website=11v11.com|access-date=June 29, 2026}} A [[2026 FIFA World Cup#Independence Day ceremonies|special ceremony]] commemorating the [[United States Semiquincentennial]], marking the 250th anniversary of the [[United States Declaration of Independence]], was held on the stadium grounds 25 minutes before kickoff.{{Cite web |title=Pre-match US Independence Day celebrations to add to FIFA World Cup 2026™ action |url=https://inside.fifa.com/organisation/news/pre-match-us-independence-day-celebrations-world-cup-2026 |access-date=5 July 2026 |website=[[FIFA]]}} In the 50th minute, Morocco took the lead when [[Azzedine Ounahi]] received the ball after a free-kick was cut-back to him from the right by [[Achraf Hakimi]], before he shot from the edge of the penalty area, low into the bottom right corner of the net.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cx2jw3e9gq0t?page=2|title= Canada 0 Morocco 3|date=4 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=7 July 2026}} Ounahi got a second goal in the 82nd minute with a finish from twelve yards out high to the top right corner of the net after a pass from [[Brahim Díaz]] from the right. [[Soufiane Rahimi]] made it 3–0 in the 98th minute with a low finish under the goalkeeper to the right corner of the net from the left of the penalty area after another pass from Díaz.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/football/canada-vs-morocco/549854|title= World Cup 2026: Canada 0–3 Morocco – Azzedine Ounahi double helps knock out co-hosts to give 2022 semi-finalists last-eight spot|date=4 July 2026|work=Sky Sports|accessdate=7 July 2026}} With this result and Egypt's loss to Argentina, Morocco was the only African team to proceed to the quarter-finals while co-host Canada was eliminated, marking its best finish since its group-stage exits in 1986 and 2022.{{cite web|url=https://www.rte.ie/sport/world-cup-2026/2026/0704/1581749-moroccos-second-half-blitz-ends-canada-journey/|title= Morocco's second-half blitz ends Canada journey|date=4 July 2026|work=RTE Sport|accessdate=7 July 2026}}

{{ill|Tomasz Kwiatkowski|pl|Tomasz Kwiatkowski (sędzia piłkarski)}} ([[Polish Football Association|Poland]]) |} ===Paraguay vs France=== Paraguay had never defeated France in their five previous encounters, including two matches at the FIFA World Cup: a [[1958 FIFA World Cup Group 2#France vs Paraguay|7–3 group stage loss]] in [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]] and a [[1998 FIFA World Cup knockout_stage#France vs Paraguay|1–0 round of 16 defeat]] via [[golden goal]] at the [[1998 FIFA World Cup]] (which France hosted and current manager [[Didier Deschamps]] also played in). Their latest meeting was a 5–0 friendly in 2017.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/paraguay/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/France/|title=Paraguay national football team: record v France|website=11v11.com|access-date=June 29, 2026}} The match was played in extreme heat, with temperatures up to {{cvt|38|C|F}} at kick-off time.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cdx7jwdy5ndt|title=Paraguay 0 France 1|date=6 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=7 July 2026}} In the 70th minute, France were awarded a penalty after a VAR intervention when substitute [[Désiré Doué]] was fouled inside the box by [[Diego Gómez (Paraguayan footballer)|Diego Gómez]]. [[Kylian Mbappé]] scored a penalty with a low shot to the right corner, sending Paraguay goalkeeper [[Orlando Gill]] the wrong way. Paraguay committed 13 fouls to France's 11 during the game, but escaped without a single yellow card while France received three.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/05/paraguay-france-last-16-world-cup-match-report|title= Mbappé and France ditch ‘tuxedos’ to play ‘dirty football’ and beat Paraguay|date=6 July 2026|work=Guardian|accessdate=7 July 2026}} Global media were almost unanimous in condemning the Paraguayan team's systematic foul play,[https://www.rfi.fr/fr/sports/20260706-football-dégoûtant-totalement-honteux-la-presse-internationale-étrille-le-paraguay-après-son-match-face-à-la-france "«Football dégoûtant», «totalement honteux»: la presse internationale étrille le Paraguay après son match face à la France"], [[Radio France internationale]], 6 July 2026 as well as [[Ilgiz Tantashev]]'s poor refereeing.[https://www.franceinfo.fr/coupe-du-monde/je-n-avais-jamais-joue-un-match-comme-ca-avec-autant-de-coups-l-arbitrage-de-france-paraguay-au-centre-des-debats-a-la-coupe-du-monde-2026_8095031.html ""Je n'avais jamais joué un match avec autant de coups, de coups en traître" : l'arbitrage de France-Paraguay au centre des débats à la Coupe du monde"], [[France Info]], 5 July 2026 Alongside the Canada vs Morocco match in Houston, a special ceremony commemorating the 250th anniversary of the United States Declaration of Independence was held on the stadium grounds 25 minutes before kickoff. After the match, Paraguayan Senator, [[Celeste Amarilla]], racially abused Mbappé on social media and the latter made a statement on X in response. Amarilla made a response which was not well received.{{cite news |title='A despicable woman': Kylian Mbappé hits out at Paraguayan senator over racist attack |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/06/a-despicable-women-kylian-mbappe-hits-out-at-paraguayan-senator-over-racist-attack |work=[[The Guardian]] |agency=[[Reuters]] |date=6 July 2026}}{{cite news |last1=Geiger |first1=Chris |title=«Noix de coco», «chimpanzé»: une sénatrice se lâche contre Mbappé |url=https://www.20min.ch/fr/story/coupe-du-monde-2026-noix-de-coco-chimpanze-une-senatrice-se-lache-contre-mbappe-103597633 |access-date=6 July 2026 |work=[[20 minutes (France)|20 minutes]] |date=6 July 2026 |language=fr}}{{cite news |last=Peter |first=Mike |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/ckg557wxrzro |title=Mbappe condemns racist posts from Paraguay senator |publisher=[[BBC Sport]] |date=6 July 2026 }}

[[Carlos del Cerro Grande]] ([[Royal Spanish Football Federation|Spain]]) |} ===Mexico vs England=== [[File:Mexico v England (2026 FIFA World Cup - Round of 16).jpg|thumb|Pre-match ceremony between Mexico and England]] The teams had previously faced each other nine times, most recently in 2010, a 3–1 win for England in a friendly match. They have also met at the [[1966 FIFA World Cup]], when England, as the host, [[1966 FIFA World Cup Group 1#England vs Mexico|won 2–0]] in the group stage.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/mexico/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/england/|title=Mexico national football team: record v England|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 1, 2026}} Additionally, these two last encountered each other at [[Estadio Azteca]] in a friendly that ended with a [[1985 Azteca 2000 Tournament|1–0 victory for Mexico in 1985]].{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/matches/mexico-v-england-09-june-1985-239281/|title=Mexico v England, 9 June 1985|website=11v11.com|access-date= July 1, 2026}} This was also the first time that England had played at Estadio Azteca since its infamous [[Argentina v England (1986 FIFA World Cup)|match against Argentina in the 1986 World Cup]], in which England famously conceded Maradona's [[The hand of God|"hand of God" goal]] and the "[[Goal of the Century]]". Before the match, it was reported that the game would be moved up to 12:00 p.m. due to reports of inclement weather and lightning. However, after protests from both Mexico and England, FIFA reversed the decision and kept the kickoff at the original time.{{cite web |last1=Mokbel |first1=Sami |last2=Kearns |first2=Sean |title=England-Mexico kick-off unchanged after Fifa U-turn |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/cvgmz3jyq9qo |publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=3 July 2026 |access-date=3 July 2026}}{{cite web |last1=Ogden |first1=Mark |title=Sources: Mexico vs. England World Cup kickoff time unchanged after talks |url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49260497/mexico-vs-england-world-cup-clash-brought-forward-six-hours-sources |publisher=[[ESPN FC]] |access-date=3 July 2026|date=3 July 2026}} An hour before the kickoff, FIFA delayed the match by one hour due to the aforementioned inclement weather.{{cite web |last1=Pitt-Brooke |first1=Jack |title=England’s World Cup clash with Mexico delayed due to bad weather: Live updates |url=https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/live-blogs/mexico-vs-england-live-updates-world-cup-2026-score-result/1kXwPh7t8O2X/ |publisher=The New York Times |access-date=5 July 2026|date=5 July 2026}} In the 36th minute, [[Jude Bellingham]] scored to put England into the lead with a diving header to the left of the net from just inside the six yard box after a cross from [[Bukayo Saka]] from the right. 98 seconds later it was 2–0 when Bellingham scored again with a low side-foot finish from just inside the six yard after a low cross form [[Harry Kane]] from the right. In the 42nd minute, [[Julián Quiñones]] pulled a goal back when he shot high to the net from eight yards out after the ball broke to him after a free-kick from the left.{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/football/england-mexico-world-cup-2026-result-highlights-goals-b3009452.html|title= England win World up thriller in Mexico to reveal true identity|date=6 July 2026|work=The Independent|accessdate=7 July 2026}} In the 54th minute, [[Jarell Quansah]] was sent off when he dived in with a high challenge on [[Jesús Gallardo]]. England were awarded a penalty in the 60th minute when [[Anthony Gordon (footballer)|Anthony Gordon]] was brought down by the diving Mexican goalkeeper [[Raúl Rangel (footballer)|Raúl Rangel]]. Kane scored the penalty with a shot to the left of the net to restore England's two goal lead.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/live/2026/jul/05/mexico-v-england-world-cup-2026-last-16-live|title= Mexico 2 England 3: Live Updates|date=6 July 2026|work=Guardian|accessdate=7 July 2026}} In the 69th minute, Mexico were awarded a penalty when Kane kicked the foot of [[Brian Gutiérrez]] in the penalty area, [[Raúl Jiménez]] scored with a shot to the left corner, but Mexico were unable to find an equaliser, with 11 minutes added on at the end.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cr5j14eyzm8t|title= Mexico 2 England 3|date=6 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=7 July 2026}} Mexico’s defeat marked the first time they had lost a World Cup match at the Azteca. It was the second co-host to be eliminated after Canada.{{cite web|url=https://lasvegassun.com/news/2026/jul/05/england-hands-mexico-its-first-world-cup-loss-at-e/|title=England hands Mexico its first World Cup loss at Estadio Azteca, winning 3–2 to reach quarterfinals|website=Las Vegas Sun|access-date= July 5, 2026}}{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49283101/england-end-mexico-world-cup-best-win-foreign-soil-their-history |title=England end Mexico's World Cup with best win on foreign soil in their history|website=[[ESPN]]|access-date= July 5, 2026}} Additionally, this was the final game played in Mexico for the tournament.

[[Juan Soto (referee)|Juan Soto]] ([[Venezuelan Football Federation|Venezuela]]) |} ===Portugal vs Spain=== [[File:AT&T Stadium World Cup.jpg|thumb|right|Pre-match ceremony between Portugal and Spain.]] {{see also|Portugal–Spain football rivalry}} The teams had faced each other in 41 previous matches including at the FIFA World Cup on two previous occasions: their first meeting was a [[2010 FIFA World Cup knockout stage#Spain_vs_Portugal|1–0 round of 16 win]] for Spain at the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]] and then a [[2018 FIFA World Cup Group B#Portugal vs Spain|3–3 group stage draw]] at the [[2018 FIFA World Cup]]. Their latest match was the [[2025 UEFA Nations League final]] in which Portugal won 5–3 on penalties following a 2–2 draw.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/portugal/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Spain/|title=Portugal national football team: record v Spain|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 2, 2026}} In the third minute, [[Mikel Oyarzabal]] dragged a shot from the left wide of the right post. In the 41st minute [[Nuno Mendes (footballer, born 2002)|Nuno Mendes]] hit the crossbar for Portugal after his shot deflected off [[Pedro Porro]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/06/portugal-spain-world-cup-last-16-match-report|title= Merino strikes at the death for Spain to send Portugal and Ronaldo limping out|date=6 July 2026|work=Guardian|accessdate=7 July 2026}} In the 91st minute substitute [[Mikel Merino]] received a pass from fellow ‌substitute [[Ferran Torres]] on the left of the penalty area before shooting low left-footed to the left corner of the net for the only goal of the match. Portugal's defeat ended [[Cristiano Ronaldo]]'s World Cup career after he had confirmed earlier that this tournament would be his last.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cze9xg6480gt|title= Portugal 0 Spain 1|date=6 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=7 July 2026}} Following the result, Portugal coach [[Roberto Martínez]] announced his resignation with immediate effect.{{Cite news |last=Beattie |first=Darren |date=6 July 2026 |title=Roberto Martinez to leave Portugal job following 2026 FIFA World Cup exit to Spain – 'Does not make sense to continue' |url=https://www.tntsports.co.uk/football/world-cup/2026/roberto-martinez-leaves-portugal-spain-defeat-last-16-cristiano-ronaldo_sto23317045/story.shtml |work=[[TNT Sports]]}} Meanwhile, Portugal striker [[Cristiano Ronaldo]] confirmed that this was his last World Cup match ahead of his expected retirement.{{Cite web |date=7 July 2026 |title=Record-setting Ronaldo says goodbye to the World Cup |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/cristiano-ronaldo-farewell |website=[[FIFA]]}}

[[Marco Di Bello]] ([[Italian Football Federation|Italy]]) |} ===United States vs Belgium=== {{See also|2026 Trump–FIFA red card controversy}} The teams had previously faced each other seven times, most recently on March 28, a 5–2 win for Belgium in a friendly. They had also met twice in the FIFA World Cup: a [[1930 FIFA World Cup Group 4#United States vs Belgium|3–0 group stage win]] for the United States at the [[1930 FIFA World Cup|first World Cup]] (their only win ever against Belgium) and a [[2014 FIFA World Cup knockout stage#Belgium vs United_States|2–1 round of 16 win]] for Belgium in [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]].{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/usa/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Belgium/|title=United States national football team: record v Belgium|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 2, 2026}} The [[2026 Trump–FIFA red card controversy|reversal of the suspension]] for [[Folarin Balogun]], who received a red card in his previous match against Bosnia and Herzegovina, has drawn condemnation of political manipulation from [[UEFA]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/02a7-2109c8e9ef81-de5a993db109-1000--uefa-statement-on-the-balogun-case/|title=UEFA statement on the Balogun case|date=July 6, 2026|website=UEFA.com}} and the [[Royal Belgian Football Association]], whose appeal was overturned.{{Cite web|url=https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/05/trump-fifa-balogun-world-cup-red-card-suspension.html|title=Balogun to start for U.S. vs. Belgium after Trump defends call to FIFA|first1=Garrett|last1=Downs|first2=Jack|last2=Sommers|first3=Dan|last3=Mangan|date=July 5, 2026|website=CNBC}} The United States was the final co-host to be eliminated, as Canada was eliminated by Morocco, followed by Mexico by England, with all occurring in the Round of 16. Balogun's participation in the match ended up being insignificant as Balogun and the rest of the United States team struggled for the majority of the match with Belgium easily beating the defense of the host team.{{Cite web |last=Tobin |first=Sam |title=Belgium end US World Cup dreams with 4–1 rout amid Balogun row |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/de-ketelaere-double-puts-belgium-into-world-cup-last-eight-ends-home-interest-2026-07-07/ |website=Reuters}}{{Cite web |last4=Yomtov |first1=Seth |last1=Vertelney |first2=Nancy |last2=Armour |first3=Jordan |last3=Mendoza |first4=Jesse |title=USA eliminated from World Cup with nightmare moment in loss to Belgium |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/soccer/worldcup/2026/07/06/usa-belgium-world-cup-live-score-usmnt-lineups-watch-highlights/90817355007/ |access-date=2026-07-07 |website=USA TODAY |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |first=Ronald |last=Blum |date=2026-07-06 |title=Belgium beats US 4–1 to reach World Cup quarterfinals, taking advantage of defensive lapses |url=https://apnews.com/article/world-cup-united-states-belgium-score-0325e8102be7a88e852079deffd70ca0 |access-date=2026-07-07 |website=AP News |language=en}} The United States registered their largest margin of defeat in a World Cup match since they lost 3–0 to the Czech Republic in [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]], and their worst goals-against performance in a World Cup match since a 5–1 defeat to Czechoslovakia in the [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990 World Cup]].{{Cite web|last=Gray |first=Ben |title=Stats show the USMNT's loss to Belgium was their worst World Cup performance in decades |url= https://fansided.com/soccer/stats-show-the-usmnt-loss-to-belgium-worst-world-cup-performance-in-decades |website=Fansided.com|date= July 7, 2026}}

[[Hernán Mastrángelo]] ([[Argentine Football Association|Argentina]]) |} ===Argentina vs Egypt=== [[File:Mostafa Shobeir Lionel Messi Argentina v Egypt 7 July 2026-167.jpg|thumb|right|[[Mostafa Shobeir]] saving the [[Lionel Messi]] penalty during the match.]] Argentina had faced Egypt twice previously, a [[Football at the 1928 Summer Olympics#Semi-finals|6–0 victory]] in the semifinals of the [[1928 Summer Olympics]], and a 2–0 friendly win in 2008.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/argentina/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Egypt/|title=Argentina national football team: record v Egypt|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 3, 2026}}{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/matches/argentina-v-egypt-06-june-1928-223814/|title=Argentina v Egypt, 06 June 1928|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 4, 2026}} In the 15th minute Egypt went in front when [[Yasser Ibrahim]] headed into the right corner of the net from six yards out after a cross from the right by [[Marwan Attia]]. Six minutes later, Argentina were awarded a penalty when [[Haissem Hassan]] tripped [[Nicolás Tagliafico]] on the right of the penalty area. [[Lionel Messi]] took the penalty, which was saved by Egyptian goalkeeper [[Mostafa Shobeir]] down low to his left.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/football/argentina-vs-egypt/549861|title=World Cup 2026: Argentina 3–2 Egypt – Lionel Messi and holders come from two down as Enzo Fernandez wins classic|date=7 July 2026|work=Sky Sports|accessdate=10 July 2026}} A second goal for Egypt, scored by [[Mostafa Ziko]] in the 60th minute after a counter-attack was ruled out after a VAR review brought it back for a foul on [[Lisandro Martínez]] at the start of the move. In the 67th minute Ziko made it 2–0 when he finished from six yards out after a low cross from [[Haissem Hassan]] from the right.{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49298199/argentina-lionel-messi-world-cup-round-16-egypt-var-mohamed-salah|title=Messi, Argentina ride VAR drama to complete epic comeback vs. Egypt|date=7 July 2026|work=ESPN|accessdate=10 July 2026}} In the 79th minute Argentina pulled a goal back when [[Cristian Romero]] headed powerfully to the right of the net from six yards out after a cross from the right from Messi. In the 83rd minute, Messi made it 2–2 when he half-volleyed the ball high in off the underside of the crossbar from twelve yards out on the right of the penalty area. In the 93rd minute, Argentina broke and [[Lautaro Martínez]] crossed from the right for [[Enzo Fernández]] to head into the right corner of the net from seven yards out to win the game 3–2 for Argentina. The winning goal was the 3,000th goal in tournament history.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cdr4m2122lgt|title=Argentina 3 Egypt 2|date=7 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=10 July 2026}}

{{ill|Bram Van Driessche|nl}} ([[Royal Belgian Football Association|Belgium]]) |} ===Switzerland vs Colombia=== The teams had met four times prior, including Colombia's [[1994 FIFA World Cup Group A#Switzerland vs Colombia|2–0 group stage victory]] at the [[1994 FIFA World Cup]]. Their most recent meeting was also won by Colombia, a 3–1 friendly in 2007.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/switzerland/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Colombia/|title=Switzerland national football team: record v Colombia|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 3, 2026}} With this game, Colombia became the only team in the 2026 World Cup to play matches in all three host countries (Canada, Mexico, and the United States).{{Cite news |last=Ramírez |first=Frida |date=6 July 2026 |title=Colombia will make history at the 2026 World Cup: it will be the only national team to have played in the United States, Mexico, and Canada |url=https://www.beinsports.com/en-us/soccer/fifa-world-cup-2026/articles/colombia-will-make-history-at-the-2026-world-cup-it-will-be-the-only-national-team-to-have-played-in-the-united-states-mexico-and-canada-2026-07-06 |access-date=7 July 2026 |work=[[beIN Sports]]}} The game finished 0–0 after extra time and went to a [[List of FIFA World Cup penalty shoot-outs|penalty shoot-out]]. Colombia's [[Davinson Sánchez]] missed his penalty when he hit the underside of the bar with the ball bouncing back out, Switzerland's [[Manuel Akanji]] also missed when he kicked high over the bar. Switzerland goalkeeper [[Gregor Kobel]] saved down to his right from [[Cucho Hernández]], with [[Rubén Vargas]] scoring the winning penalty with a shot low to the left, sending the goalkeeper the wrong way.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/08/switzerland-colombia-world-cup-2026-last-16-match-report|title=Switzerland knock out Colombia on penalties to reach World Cup quarter-finals|date=7 July 2026|work=Guardian|accessdate=8 July 2026}} Switzerland reached the quarter-finals for the fourth time (after [[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]], [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]], and [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]), their first in 72 years (since [[Austria v Switzerland (1954 FIFA World Cup)|1954]], when it hosted the tournament).{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cx2vrg8gzr1t|title= Switzerland 0 Colombia 0 (4–3 on pens)|date=7 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=8 July 2026}} Colombia's elimination from the tournament confirms that Argentina becomes the sole representative from the [[Americas]] to proceed to the quarterfinals following their win against Egypt.{{Cite web|url=https://www.beinsports.com/en-us/soccer/fifa-world-cup-2026/articles/america-disappoints-at-the-2026-world-cup-only-argentina-survives-as-the-rest-of-the-continent-suffers-a-historic-failure-2026-07-07|last=Ramírez|first=Frida Samantha |title=America disappoints at the 2026 World Cup: only Argentina survives as the rest of the continent suffers a historic failure|date= July 7, 2026|website=[[beIN Sports]]}} This was the final game of the tournament played in Canada, as matches for the remaining knockout stages (quarterfinals, semifinals, match for third place and final) will be played entirely in the United States.

[[Ivan Bebek]] ([[Croatian Football Federation|Croatia]]) |} ==Quarterfinals== ===France vs Morocco=== The two teams had met six times before, with France winning four matches. Their most recent meeting took place in the [[2022 FIFA World Cup knockout stage#France vs Morocco|semi-finals of the last World Cup]], when France won 2–0.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/france/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Morocco/|title= France national football team: record v Morocco|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 4, 2026}} In the 28th minute, France were awarded a penalty when [[Kylian Mbappé]] was fouled inside the penalty area on the left by [[Noussair Mazraoui]]. After a delay of over three minutes, Mbappé took the penalty and shot low to the right which was saved by Moroccan goalkeeper [[Yassine Bounou]]. [[Désiré Doué]] also had a low shot to the left from inside the penalty area saved by Bounou.{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49319513/france-coach-deschamps-unhappy-wait-missed-mbappe-pen|title=France coach Deschamps unhappy with wait on missed Mbappé pen|date=9 July 2026|work=ESPN|accessdate=10 July 2026}} In first-half stoppage time [[Lucas Digne]]'s hit a long range swerving shot from the left off the top of the crossbar and go over.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/football/france-vs-morocco/report/549862|title=World Cup 2026: France 2–0 Morocco – Kylian Mbappe magic decides quarter-final after his own early penalty miss|date=9 July 2025|work=Sky Sport|accessdate=10 July 2026}} In the 60th minute, France went in front when Mbappé received the ball from Doué on the left before curling a shot from just inside the penalty area into the top right corner of the net, it was his eighth goal of the World Cup. Six minutes later it was 2–0 when [[Ousmane Dembélé]] scored with a low shot from just outside the penalty area to the right corner which Bounou got a hand to but couldn't keep out. Morocco were missing injured forward [[Ismael Saibari]] and failed to get a shot on target until the 84th minute.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c30y244nl8mt#Report|title=France 2 Morocco 0|date=9 July 2025|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=10 July 2026}}

[[Joe Dickerson]] ([[United States Soccer Federation|United States]]) |} ===Norway vs England=== The teams had previously faced each other 12 times, most recently in 2014, a 1–0 win for England in a friendly match.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/norway/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/England/|title=Norway national football team: record v England|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 5, 2026}} Norway went ahead after 36 minutes when [[Andreas Schjelderup|Andreas Schjelderup's]] left-footed strike from just inside the penalty area went over England goalkeeper [[Jordan Pickford]]. England restored parity in first half stoppage time when [[Jude Bellingham]] took a pass from Anthony Gordon and struck past Norway goalkeeper [[Ørjan Nyland]].{{Cite web |website=BBC Sport| last=McNulty |first=Phil |date=11 Jul 2026 |title=Norway 1-2 England: Jude Bellingham scores twice as Three Lions reach World Cup semi-final - BBC Sport |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cgl33l55100t}} [[Declan Rice]], who had been struggling with illness and injury leading into the fixture, was substituted at half-time, as well as Noni Madueke, being replaced with [[Eberechi Eze]] and [[Bukayo Saka]]. In the second half, a goal by Norway midfielder [[Torbjørn Heggem]] was ruled out due to [[Erling Haaland]] fouling midfielder Elliot Anderson, and [[David Møller Wolfe|David Møller Wolfe's]] header struck the bar. In extra time, Bellingham scored the second goal for England when Nyland parried a shot towards him from substitute Morgan Rogers. The Norwegians claimed the ball struck one of the [[Spidercam]] wires in the build-up to Bellingham's equalising goal, but [[Snickometer]] ruled there was no contact. Had the decision been made that the ball struck the wire, the goal would have been ruled out and play restarted with a dropped ball.{{Cite web |website=BBC Sport| last=Rajan |first=Adwaidh |date=12 July 2026 |title=Did ball hit spidercam before England goal? Snicko says not - Norway unsure |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/cd95335dp10o}} [[Jude Bellingham]]'s two goals meant this England side were the first in [[FIFA World Cup]] history to have two players score six or more goals in a tournament, with [[Harry Kane]] having reached six in the previous match against [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]]. Also, Bellingham's six goals in the tournament, none of which were penalties, is the joint-most non-penalty goals by an England player in a major men's tournament, along with [[Gary Lineker]] in the [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986 edition of the tournament]].{{Cite web |website=England Football| last=Hunt |first=Holly |date=12 July 2026 |title=Norway 1-2 England stats: Three Lions match winning streak |url=https://www.englandfootball.com/articles/2026/Jul/12/england-norway-post-match-stats-world-cup-quarter-final-20261207}}

[[Carlos del Cerro Grande]] ([[Royal Spanish Football Federation|Spain]]) |} ===Argentina vs Switzerland=== [[File:Argentina v Switzerland.jpg|thumb|right|Pre-match ceremony between Argentina and Switzerland.]] This was Switzerland's first World Cup quarterfinal since the [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954 edition of the tournament]].{{Cite web |website=ESPN Sport|date=12 July 2026 |title=Argentina survive Switzerland scare to book semifinal with England |url=https://www.espn.com.au/football/report/_/gameId/760513}} Argentina had never lost to Switzerland in their seven previous encounters, winning five of those matches. Their first meeting took place at the [[1966 FIFA World Cup]], when Argentina [[1966 FIFA World Cup Group 2#Argentina vs Switzerland|won 2–0 in the group stage]] and their latest match was also at the World Cup, a [[2014 FIFA World Cup knockout_stage#Argentina vs Switzerland|round of 16 1–0 win (after extra time)]] in [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]].{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/argentina/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Switzerland//|title=Argentina national football team: record v Switzerland|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 7, 2026}} [[Alexis Mac Allister]] opened the scoring for Argentina after 10 minutes when he scored off a corner kick taken from [[Lionel Messi]]. Half way through the second half, shortly after [[Dan Ndoye]] scored the equalizer for Switzerland, [[Leandro Paredes]] was shown a yellow card for a tackle on [[Breel Embolo]]–however, [[Video assistant referee|VAR]] revealed Embolo falling before Paredes made any contact. Since Embolo had already received a booking, he was sent off. It was the second time a yellow card had been overturned at the tournament due to the "mistaken identity" protocol. The match ended [[Lionel Messi]]'s record run of scoring in nine consecutive [[FIFA World Cup]] matches, stretching back to the Round of 16 match against [[Australia men's national football team|Australia]] in [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]].

[[Marco Di Bello]] ([[Italian Football Federation|Italy]]) |} ==Semifinals== This is the first time in tournament history that the top four teams in the [[FIFA Men's World Ranking]] as of June 2026 have all qualified for the World Cup semifinals.{{cite web |title=Top four in FIFA/Coca Cola World Ranking set for historic showdown |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/top-four-fifa-coca-cola-ranking-semi-final |publisher=FIFA |date=July 12, 2026 |access-date=July 14, 2026 |archive-date=July 14, 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260714175811/https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/top-four-fifa-coca-cola-ranking-semi-final |url-status=live }} It's also only the third time in tournament history (after [[1970 FIFA World Cup knockout stage|1970]] and [[1990 FIFA World Cup knockout stage|1990]]) that all four semifinalists are previous champions. ===France vs Spain=== {{see also|France–Spain football rivalry}} [[File:Aymeric Laporte France v Spain 7.24.26-061.jpg|thumb|right|During the match between France and Spain.]] France and Spain had faced each other in 38 previous matches since 1922, including the [[UEFA Euro 1984 final]] (when host France won 2–0) and a [[2006 FIFA World Cup knockout stage#Spain vs France|3–1 round of 16 win]] for France at the [[2006 FIFA World Cup]]. Their latest encounter took place at the [[2025 UEFA Nations League Finals]], when Spain [[2025 UEFA Nations League Finals#Spain vs France|won 5–4 in the semifinals]].{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/france/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Spain/|title= France national football team: record v Spain|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 9, 2026}} In the 22nd minute, Spain was awarded a penalty when [[Lucas Digne]] kicked [[Lamine Yamal]] with his swinging left leg when trying to clear the ball. [[Mikel Oyarzabal]] scored the penalty with a powerful shot to the right of the net to make it 1–0. In the 58th minute it was 2–0 when [[Pedro Porro]] played a one-two with [[Dani Olmo]] on the edge of the penalty area before a side-foot finish with his right foot to the right corner of the net from the right of the penalty area.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/14/france-spain-world-cup-semi-final-match-report|title=Spain reach World Cup final after Oyarzabal and Porro sink sorry France|date=14 July 2026|work=Guardian|accessdate=16 July 2026}} The game finished 2–0 and Spain progressed to its second World Cup final.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/cj3gdp4d3nnt|title=France 0 Spain 2|date=14 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=16 July 2026}}

Guillermo Pacheco ([[Mexican Football Federation|Mexico]]) |} ===England vs Argentina=== {{see also|Argentina–England football rivalry}} The teams had faced each other in 14 previous matches since 1951, with five of those encounters taking place at the World Cup: a [[1962 FIFA World Cup Group 4#England vs Argentina|3–1 group stage win]] for England in [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]], a [[1966 FIFA World Cup knockout stage#England vs Argentina|1–0 quarterfinal win]] for England (as host) in [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]], a [[Argentina v England (1986 FIFA World Cup)|2–1 quarterfinal win]] for Argentina in [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]] (a match famous for [[Diego Maradona]]'s [[The hand of God|"hand of God" goal]] and the "[[Goal of the Century]]"), a [[1998 FIFA World Cup knockout stage#Argentina vs England|2–2 round of 16 draw (Argentina won 4–3 on penalties)]] in [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]], and a [[2002 FIFA World Cup Group F#Argentina vs England|1–0 group stage win]] for England in [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]. Their latest match took place in 2005, a 3–2 win for England in a friendly match.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/england/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Argentina/|title=England national football team: record v Argentina|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 11, 2026}} After a goalless first half, [[Anthony Gordon (footballer)|Anthony Gordon]] put England into the lead in the 55th minute when he finished to the left of the net with his right foot from six yards out after a low cross in by [[Morgan Rogers]] from the right. As England looked to hold on to its lead, it went more defensive and switched to a back five. Argentina created chances after that with [[Jordan Pickford]] saving down low on the line from a close-range header by [[Nicolás González (footballer, born 1998)|Nicolás González]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11095/13562935/world-cup-2026-england-1-2-argentina-three-lions-suffer-semi-final-loss-after-stunning-late-turnaround-by-holders|title= World Cup 2026: England 1-2 Argentina - Three Lions suffer semi-final loss after stunning late turnaround by holders|date=15 July 2026|work=Sky Sports|accessdate=16 July 2026}} [[Alexis Mac Allister]] then saw his diving header from close range strike the left-side post. In the 85th minute, [[Enzo Fernández]] received a pass from [[Lionel Messi]] just outside the penalty area before shooting right-footed to the left corner of the net to make it 1–1.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/15/england-argentina-world-cup-semi-final-match-report|title= Argentina’s late double breaks England hearts in dramatic World Cup semi-final|date=15 July 2026|work=Guardian|accessdate=16 July 2026}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/ce8l15903k8o|title= 'Passive' and 'crumbled' - did Tuchel's defensive tactics cost England|date=15 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=16 July 2026}} In the 92nd minute, Mac Allister saw his low shot from the left hit the right hand post before coming back to Messi, who crossed from the right for [[Lautaro Martínez]] to head into the left corner from three yards out to give Argentina a 2–1 lead. Argentina saw out the remainder of the added time to advance to the [[2026 FIFA World Cup final|final]] against [[Spain national football team|Spain]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c77yp11e4r6t?page=2|title= England 1 Argentina 2|date=15 July 2026|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=16 July 2026}} England's defeat ended its hopes of reaching the final and extended its period without a championship to 14 consecutive tournament appearances since [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]].{{Cite news |last=McNulty |first=Phil |date=16 July 2026 |title=Why England's loss to Argentina felt like most painful in 60 years of hurt |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/cj365n05mgdo |access-date=16 July 2026 |work=[[BBC Sport]]}} However, England progressed to the third-place playoff against [[France national football team|France]], marking its first appearance in the bronze-medal match since [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]. England manager [[Thomas Tuchel]] was widely criticized after the match for going too defensive after England took a 1–0 lead with over 30 minutes remaining. Between England scoring and Martínez's winning goal, England had just 12% possession.{{Cite web |last=Olley |first=James |date=July 15, 2026 |title=Thomas Tuchel vows to stay as England boss, defends tactics vs. Argentina |url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49371265/thomas-tuchel-england-argentina-tactics-2026-world-cup-future}} After the defeat, Tuchel remarked that he fully intended to stay on as England manager, and proceeded to blame the country's "football DNA" for its World Cup exit.{{Cite web |website=Fox Sports|date=17 July 2026 |title=Bombshell dressing room reveal after WC blunder as England coach blames ‘weak DNA’ |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/football/fifa-world-cup-2026-thomas-tuchel-blames-englands-football-dna-for-semifinal-loss-to-argentina-reports-players-stunned-by-tuchels-tactics-will-tuchel-be-sacked-as-england-manager/news-story/c832fa31e8dfa214b1d968431d46ac8e}} After the match, some Argentine players displayed a banner that read ''"Las Malvinas son Argentinas"'' ("[[Falkland Islands|The Falkland Islands]] are Argentinian") and later left it on the pitch, in apparent contravention of FIFA rules.{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2026/jul/15/malvinas-is-argentinian-world-cup-holders-celebrate-win-over-england-with-banner|title=‘Malvinas are Argentine’: World Cup holders celebrate win over England with Falklands banner|access-date=16 July 2026|website=The Guardian}} FIFA's ''Stadium Code of Conduct'' prohibits banners and related paraphernalia that are of a political or discriminatory nature inside stadiums,{{Cite web |website=ABC News |date=16 July 2026 |title=World Cup quick hits: England boss Thomas Tuchel's tactics allow Lionel Messi to orchestrate Argentina win |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-07-16/world-cup-quick-hits-england-argentina-messi-tuchel/106920846}} and the British government immediately urged FIFA to investigate the incident.{{Cite web |website=ESPN|date=17 July 2026 |title=UK urges FIFA investigation of Argentina over Falklands banner |url=https://www.espn.com.au/football/story/_/id/49375290/argentina-inappropriate-falklands-banner-england-world-cup-clash-minister}} In a statement after the game, FIFA said: "As is standard procedure, FIFA's independent Disciplinary Committee is currently assessing the match reports and considering the relevant circumstances before deciding on potential further steps based on the FIFA Disciplinary Code." Argentina was fined by FIFA in 2014 after holding up a banner with the same slogan at the conclusion of a friendly game against Slovenia.

The first two meetings took place at the group stage and were won by England: [[1966 FIFA World Cup Group 1#England vs France|2–0]] as host in [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] and [[1982 FIFA World Cup Group 4#England vs France|3–1]] in [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]. [[Carlos del Cerro Grande]] ([[Royal Spanish Football Federation|Spain]]) |} ==Final== {{main|2026 FIFA World Cup final}} Spain and Argentina have faced each other in 14 previous matches since 1952, with an even record as both teams won six matches. They only met once at the World Cup, when Argentina [[1966 FIFA World Cup Group 2#Argentina vs Spain|won 2–1]] at the group stage of the [[1966 FIFA World Cup]]. Their latest meeting took place in 2018, a 6–1 win for Spain in a friendly.{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/spain/tab/opposingTeams/opposition/Argentina/|title=Spain national football team: record v Argentina|website=11v11.com|access-date=July 14, 2026}} They were expected to meet at the [[2026 Finalissima]] on March 27 at the [[Lusail Stadium]] in [[Lusail]], [[Qatar]].
